title: Yellow Level 2 weather warning: Damaging waves
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - A cold front...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - A cold front will impact the Western Cape on Thursday morning, 17 July, bringing south-westerly waves of 4.5 to 6.0 meters.


> This will result in damaging waves affecting areas from Plettenberg Bay to Saldanha Bay on Thursday afternoon, spreading to Alexander Bay on Friday, 18 July.


Windy conditions (40-60 km/h) are expected along the south-western and southern coastlines on Thursday afternoon.

Affected Areas: Western Cape and Namaqua  Impacts:

- The public is at risk of being swept off coastal rocks due to the frequent occurrence of large waves.
- Small vessels are at risk of capsizing in navigation at sea.
- Localized disruptions to port and harbour activities for a short period.
- Localized disruptions to beachfront activities are also likely.

Safety Advice: Stay clear of coastal areas and follow the guidance of local authorities.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
